The 2019 season saw the '''[[Wigan Warriors]]''' compete in the [[Super League XXIV|Super League]], Grand Final Playoffs, [[2019 Challenge Cup|Challenge Cup]], and the [[2019 World Club Challenge|World Club Challenge]].

==World Club Challenge==
{{main|2019 World Club Challenge}}
Winners of the [[2018 Super League Grand Final]], Wigan Warriors qualified to play [[2018 NRL season|2018 NRL]] champions [[Sydney Roosters]] in the [[2019 World Club Challenge]]. Wigan were beaten by the Australian side, retaining the title for the NRL.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/47217076|title=Wigan Warriors 8-20 Sydney Roosters: NRL side win World Club Challenge|date=February 17, 2019|via=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>

==Super League==
{{main|Super League XXIV}}

=== Matches ===
Wigan's form was split through the season. The previous year's [[2018 Super League Grand Final|Grand Final]] champions start the season in poor form winning only once in their first six games. Form improved towards the middle of the season, before a surge of good results towards the end of the season – losing only once in the last three months. Wigan finished second in the league for the second year in a row.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.seriousaboutrl.com/bounce-back-wigan-warriors-2019-season-review-23571/|title=Bounce Back?! Wigan Warriors 2019 Season Review|date=October 23, 2019}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/teams/wigan/scores-fixtures|title=Wigan Warriors - Scores & Fixtures - Rugby League|website=BBC Sport}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/competitions/super-league/results/2019-20|title = Super League (Sky Sports)}}</ref>

=== League table ===
{{Super League XXIV regular season table|showteam=WIG}}

===Grand Final Playoffs===
{{main|Super League XXIV#Playoffs}}
Finishing second in the league, Wigan qualified for the [[2019 Super League Grand Final|Grand Final]] Play-offs. The Warriors won the qualifying final, but lost in the semifinal and the preliminary final to finish third in the play-off series.

==Challenge Cup==
{{main|2019 Challenge Cup}}
As a "Super 8s" team of the [[Super League XXIII|2018 Super League]], Wigan Warriors entered the [[2019 Challenge Cup]] in the sixth round where they were subsequently knocked out by [[Warrington Wolves]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/challenge-cup/scores-fixtures/2019-05|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191212000814/https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/challenge-cup/scores-fixtures/2019-05|url-status=dead|archive-date=12 December 2019|title=Coral Challenge Cup - Scores & Fixtures - Rugby League|website=BBC Sport}}</ref>
